channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabili...

item.title: Fortanix helps enterprises build resilience with multi-sourced quantum entropy
item.description: Fortanix announced a new multi-sourced quantum entropy capability within Fortanix Data Security Manager (DSM), enabling enterprises to diversify encryption key generation at the origin of trust. Through partnerships w...

item.title: BlackSanta EDR-Killer Targets HR Teams in CV-Themed Campaign
item.description: BlackSanta malware targets HR staff with fake resumes, kills EDR and steals system data

item.title: Network Map 2.0 provides live network mapping and faster risk containment
item.description: Zero Networks has announced Network Map 2.0, an advancement in real-time network mapping designed to help large enterprises eliminate decision paralysis, reduce blast radius and turn visibility into immediate, enforce...

item.title: Wiz Joins Google Cloud as Landmark Acquisition Closes
item.description: Google has completed its $32 billion acquisition of the cloud security giant, which will maintain its brand. The post Wiz Joins Google Cloud as Landmark Acquisition Closes appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: CISO Conversations: Aimee Cardwell
item.description: Aimee Cardwell started her career at Netscape, become a VP of engineering at American Express, CISO at UnitedHealth Group, and now CISO in Residence at Transcend. The post CISO Conversations: Aimee Cardwell appeared f...

item.title: Researchers Discover Major Security Gaps in LLM Guardrails
item.description: Palo Alto Networks’ Unit 42 has developed a successful attack to bypass safety guardrails in popular generative AI tools

item.title: 238,000 Impacted by Bell Ambulance Data Breach
item.description: Hackers stole personal information such as names, Social Security numbers, and driver’s license numbers. The post 238,000 Impacted by Bell Ambulance Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Meta adds new WhatsApp, Facebook, and Messenger anti-scam tools
item.description: Meta is introducing new anti-scam protections across its platforms, deploying systems and user-facing warnings to protect users against scammers. [...]

item.title: Vicarius vIntelligence brings continuous risk validation and AI-driven security automation
item.description: Vicarius has announced the launch of vIntelligence, a new product that introduces agentic intelligence and continuous validation to the company’s security portfolio. With this release, Vicarius becomes a two-product c...

item.title: Scanner Raises $22 Million for AI-Powered Threat Hunting
item.description: The company connects AI agents to security data lakes for interactive investigations, detection engineering, and autonomous response. The post Scanner Raises $22 Million for AI-Powered Threat Hunting appeared first on...

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K., Canada, Korea,...

item.title: AI Security for Apps is now generally available
item.description: Cloudflare AI Security for Apps is now generally available, providing a security layer to discover and protect AI-powered applications, regardless of the model or hosting provider. We are also making AI discovery free...

item.title: Slashing agent token costs by 98% with RFC 9457-compliant error responses
item.description: Cloudflare now returns RFC 9457-compliant structured Markdown and JSON error payloads to AI agents, replacing heavyweight HTML pages with machine-readable instructions. This reduces token usage by over 98%, turning br...

item.title: Meta turns to AI to sniff out scams on Facebook, Messenger and WhatsApp
item.description: Meta’s new tools on Facebook, Messenger, and WhatsApp protect users from scams. They use advanced AI systems to analyze text, images, and surrounding context and identify sophisticated scam patterns. Facebook alerts f...

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Two Publicly Disclosed Zero-Days
item.description: March Patch Tuesday sees Microsoft release updates for 79 flaws

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rated Critical, and...

item.title: Armis improves vulnerability accuracy and speed with unified real-time visibility
item.description: Armis has announced Armis Centrix for Vulnerability Management Detection and Response. The solution enables security teams to identify and validate vulnerabilities across all organizational assets in real time. Armis’...

item.title: KadNap bot compromises 14,000+ devices to route malicious traffic
item.description: KadNap malware infects 14,000+ edge devices, mainly Asus routers, turning them into a stealth proxy botnet used to route malicious internet traffic. KadNap malware infects more than 14,000 edge devices, mainly ASUS ro...

item.title: Virtana enables full-stack root cause analysis beyond legacy APM
item.description: Virtana has launched an Application Observability offering that traces performance issues from application code through infrastructure, networks, storage, and AI workloads to deliver evidence-based root cause analysis...

item.title: Forescout replaces manual audits with automated, always-on compliance validation
item.description: Forescout Technologies has announced Automated Security Controls Assessment, a new Forescout 4D Platform capability that continuously evaluates trust, control effectiveness and compliance posture across an organizatio...

item.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours
item.description: A threat actor known as UNC6426 leveraged keys stolen following the supply chain compromise of the nx npm package last year to completely breach a victim's cloud environment within a span of 72 hours. The attack start...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Highlights Cloud Resilience Gaps
item.description: Data centers — used by both governments and militaries for operations — are now fair game, not just for cyberattacks, but for kinetic attacks as well.

item.title: 'Overly Permissive' Salesforce Cloud Configs in the Crosshairs
item.description: Some customers have mishandled guest user configurations otherwise intended to allow third-party access to important — and sensitive — client data.

item.title: AWS European Sovereign Cloud achieves first compliance milestone: SOC 2 and C5 reports plus seven ISO certifications
item.description: In January 2026, we announced the general availability of the AWS European Sovereign Cloud, a new, independent cloud for Europe entirely located within the European Union (EU), and physically and logically separate fr...

item.title: New 'Zombie ZIP' technique lets malware slip past security tools
item.description: A new technique dubbed "Zombie ZIP" helps conceal payloads in compressed files specially created to avoid detection from security solutions such as antivirus and endpoint detection and response (EDR) products. [...]

item.title: Attackers exploit FortiGate devices to access sensitive network information
item.description: Attackers are exploiting FortiGate devices to breach networks and steal configuration data containing service account credentials and network details. SentinelOne researchers warn that attackers are exploiting vulnera...

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description: After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.

item.title: Cloud Attackers Now Prefer Vulnerability Exploits Over Credentials, Google Cloud Finds
item.description: Google Cloud report details a sharp rise in attackers exploiting software vulnerabilities, including React2Shell

item.title: Microsoft brings phishing-resistant Windows sign-ins via Entra passkeys
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra on Windows devices, adding phishing-resistant passwordless authentication via Windows Hello. [...]

item.title: APT28 conducts long-term espionage on Ukrainian forces using custom malware
item.description: APT28 used BEARDSHELL and COVENANT malware to spy on Ukrainian military personnel, enabling long-term surveillance since April 2024. The Russia-linked group APT28 (aka UAC-0001, aka Fancy Bear, Pawn Storm, Sofacy Grou...

item.title: New KadNap botnet hijacks ASUS routers to fuel cybercrime proxy network
item.description: A newly discovered botnet malware called KadNap is targeting ASUS routers and other edge networking devices to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]

item.title: Ericsson Breach Exposes Data of 15k Employees and Customers
item.description: Ericsson data breach affects 15k employees/customers after third-party service provider compromise

item.title: AWS Security Hub is expanding to unify security operations across multicloud environments
item.description: After talking with many customers, one thing is clear: the security challenge has not gotten easier. Enterprises today operate across a complex mix of environments, including on-premises infrastructure, private data c...

item.title: The New Turing Test: How Threats Use Geometry to Prove 'Humanness'
item.description: Malware is evolving to evade sandboxes by pretending to be a real human behind the keyboard. The Picus Red Report 2026 shows 80% of top attacker techniques now focus on evasion and persistence, including geometry-base...

item.title: 'BlackSanta' EDR Killer Targets HR Workflows
item.description: A campaign by Russian-speaking cyberattackers hijacks workflows to deliver security-busting malware, allowing attackers to steal data without detection.

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and exfiltrate sensitive data w...

item.title: Investigating multi-vector attacks in Log Explorer
item.description: Log Explorer customers can now identify and investigate multi-vector attacks. Log Explorer supports 14 additional Cloudflare datasets, enabling users to have a 360-degree view of their network.

item.title: Building a security overview dashboard for actionable insights
item.description: Cloudflare's new Security Overview dashboard transforms overwhelming security data into prioritized, actionable insights, empowering defenders with contextual intelligence on vulnerabilities.

item.title: Ericsson US confirms breach after third-party provider attack
item.description: Ericsson US reports a data breach after attackers hacked a service provider, exposing employee and customer information. Ericsson Inc., the U.S. branch of the Swedish telecom giant, disclosed a data breach after a ser...

item.title: Law enforcement disrupted Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform
item.description: Authorities disrupted the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform used to send millions of phishing emails to over 500,000 orgs worldwide. The joint effort, led by Microsoft, Europol, and industry partners, aimed to...

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an open-source tool cal...

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability l...

item.title: Translating risk insights into actionable protection: leveling up security posture with Cloudflare and Mastercard
item.description: Cloudflare will be integrating Mastercard’s RiskRecon attack surface intelligence capabilities to help you eliminate Internet-facing blind spots while continuously monitoring and closing security gaps.

item.title: March 2026 Patch Tuesday: Eight Critical Vulnerabilities and Two Publicly Disclosed Among 82 CVEs Patched
'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
item.description: A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces.

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partners are defining th...

item.title: New Attack Against Wi-Fi
item.description: It’s called AirSnitch : Unlike previous Wi-Fi attacks, AirSnitch exploits core features in Layers 1 and 2 and the failure to bind and synchronize a client across these and higher layers, other nodes, and other network...

item.title: TriZetto Provider Solutions Breach Hits 3.4 Million Patients
item.description: Billing services provider TriZetto Provider Solutions has begun notifying millions of patients about a data breach

item.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
item.description: Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code, and harvest sensitive...

item.title: Ghanaian Pleads Guilty to Role in $100m Romance Scam
item.description: Derrick Van Yeboah admitted he stole over $10m in romance scams as part of crime gang

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. B...

item.title: North Korean APTs Use AI to Enhance IT Worker Scams
item.description: DPRK worker scams are old hat, but they're still working, thanks to AI tools that help with everything from face swapping to daily emails.

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is in and Anthropic is out as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the wealthiest titans of t...

item.title: Zero‑Day Attacks on Enterprise Software Reach Record High, Google Warns
item.description: Almost a quarter of the zero days detected by Google in 2025 targeted security and networking appliances

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.description: An unknown hacker used Anthropic’s LLM to hack the Mexican government: The unknown Claude user wrote Spanish-language prompts for the chatbot to act as an elite hacker, finding vulnerabilities in government networks,...

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.

item.title: AWS completes the 2026 annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed the annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit to operate as a Tier 1 Cloud Service Provider (CSP) for the AWS Middle East...

item.title: AI-Driven Insider Risk Now a “Critical Business Threat,” Report Warns
item.description: Malicious insiders are using misusing AI for nefarious gain, while employees cutting corners also creates risk, warns Mimecast

item.title: Coruna Exploit Kit Targets Older iPhones in Multi-Stage Campaigns
item.description: Exploit kit "Coruna" targets iPhones running iOS 13.0 to 17.2.1, focusing on financial data theft

item.title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2017-7921 Hikvision Multiple Products Improper Authentication Vulnerability...

item.title: Zero-Click FreeScout Bug Enables Remote Code Execution
item.description: Ox Security warns that Mail2Shell could enable threat actors to hijack FreeScout systems without user interaction

item.title: Cisco Issues Patches for 48 Vulnerabilities in Enterprise Networking Products
item.description: Two of the 48 Cisco vulnerabilities, affecting Secure Firewall Management Center, are maximum-severity flaws

item.title: Europol Operation Seizes LeakBase Data Breach Site
item.description: A global operation has resulted in the takedown of popular cybercrime forum LeakBase

item.title: 2025 ISO and CSA STAR certificates are now available with one additional service and one new region
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) successfully completed the annual recertification audit with no findings for ISO 9001:2015, 27001:2022, 27017:2015, 27018:2019, 27701:2019, 20000-1:2018, 22301:2019, and Cloud Security Allian...

item.title: Stranger Things Meets Cybersecurity: Lessons from the Hive Mind
item.description: Events and concepts from the Stranger Things television series illustrate how enterprises can defend their networks and stay "right side up."

item.title: Coalition of Western Countries Launches 6G Cybersecurity Guidelines
item.description: A coalition of seven Western nations has launched guidelines to help integrate security-by-design principles into future 6G standards

item.title: Global Takedown Neutralizes Tycoon2FA Phishing Service
item.description: Law enforcers and industry partners have taken down notorious phishing-as-a-service platform Tycoon2FA

item.title: Always-on detections: eliminating the WAF “log versus block” trade-off
item.description: Cloudflare is introducing Attack Signature Detection and Full-Transaction Detection to provide continuous, high-fidelity security insights without the manual tuning of traditional WAFs. By correlating request payloads...

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million, and the decrypti...

item.title: Manipulating AI Summarization Features
item.description: Microsoft is reporting : Companies are embedding hidden instructions in “Summarize with AI” buttons that, when clicked, attempt to inject persistence commands into an AI assistant’s memory via URL prompt parameters…....

item.title: Calls for Global Digital Estate Standard as Posthumous Deepfake Fraud Risk Grows
item.description: The OpenID Foundation warns that fragmented policies on posthumous digital accounts could open the door for fraudsters to exploit AI deepfakes

item.title: Indian APT 'Sloppy Lemming' Targets Defense, Critical Infrastructure
item.description: India-nexus cyber threat actors are growing more active and sophisticated, using custom tools coded in Rust and cloud-based command and control.

item.title: 2025 PiTuKri ISAE 3000 Type II attestation report available with 183 services in scope
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce the issuance of the Criteria to Assess the Information Security of Cloud Services (PiTuKri) Type II attestation report with 183 services in scope. The Finnish Transport...

item.title: Israel: RedAlert Spyware Campaign Exploits Wartime Panic With Trojanized App
item.description: Espionage campaign exploits Israel-Iran conflict, distributing a trojanized Red Alert app via SMS

item.title: On Moltbook
item.description: The MIT Technology Review has a good article on Moltbook, the supposed AI-only social network: Many people have pointed out that a lot of the viral comments were in fact posted by people posing as bots. But even the b...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-21385 Qualcomm Multiple Chipsets Memory Corruption Vulnerability CVE-202...

item.title: Huge “Shadow Layer” of Organizations Hit by Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Black Kite reveals 26,000 unnamed corporate victims linked to 136 third-party breaches

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Actor Targets Iraqi Government Officials in AI-Powered Campaign
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz assessed with medium to high confidence that an Iranian adversary targeted Iraq’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a new cyber-attack

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.

item.title: Understanding IAM for Managed AWS MCP Servers
item.description: As AI agents become part of your development workflows on Amazon Web Services (AWS), you want them to work with your existing AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions, not force you to build a separate per...

item.title: Expect Iran to Launch Cyber-Attacks Globally, Warns Google Head of Threat Intel
item.description: John Hultquist suggests “aggressive” Iranian cyber attackers will target the US and its Gulf allies with plausibly deniable ransomware attacks, hacktivist campaigns and more

item.title: Quantum-Resistant Data Diode Secures Sensitive Data on Edge Devices, Critical Systems
item.description: Forward Edge-AI's new Isidore Quantum is a compact, low-power hardware device designed to defend sensitive operational technology endpoints against future quantum attacks.

item.title: ClawJacked Bug Enables Covert AI Agent Hijacking
item.description: Oasis Security reveals how a new ClawJacked vulnerability could allow attackers to silently take over a victim’s OpenClaw agent

item.title: Taming Agentic Browsers: Vulnerability in Chrome Allowed Extensions to Hijack New Gemini Panel
item.description: A high-severity CVE-2026-0628 in Chrome's Gemini allowed local file access and privacy invasion. Google quickly patched the flaw. The post Taming Agentic Browsers: Vulnerability in Chrome Allowed Extensions to Hijack...

item.title: Ransomware Payments Decline 8% as Attacks Surge 50%
item.description: Chainalysis reveals a big surge in median ransomware payment size in 2025 despite overall drop in criminal revenue

item.title: Bug in Google's Gemini AI Panel Opens Door to Hijacking
item.description: Attackers could have exploited the vulnerability to escalate privileges, violate user privacy while browsing, and access sensitive resources.

item.title: CrowdStrike Achieves NCSC CIR Assurance for Incident Response
Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
item.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control...

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
item.description: HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.

item.title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
item.description: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts.

item.title: Flaw-Finding AI Assistants Face Criticism for Speed, Accuracy
item.description: Using AI to find security vulnerabilities holds significant promise, but the initial products fall short of the needs of enterprises and software developers, say experts.

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Expands Toolkit to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The security researchers from Zscaler ThreatLabz have also discovered five new tools deployed by the North Korean hacking group

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.

item.title: UK Vulnerability Monitoring Service Cuts Unresolved Security Flaws by 75%
item.description: The UK government says its new Vulnerability Monitoring Service has cut unresolved security flaws by 75% and reduced cyber-attack fix times from nearly two months to just over a week

item.title: ‘Project Compass’ Cracks Down on ‘The Com’: 30 Members of Notorious Cybercrime Gang Arrested
item.description: International law enforcement operation led by Europol targets network of teenagers and young adults involved in ransomware attacks, extortion and other crimes

item.title: AWS successfully completed its first surveillance audit for ISO 42001:2023 with no findings
item.description: In November 2024, Amazon Web Services (AWS) was the first major cloud service provider to announce the ISO/IEC 42001 accredited certification for AI services, covering: Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Q Business, Amazon Textra...

item.title: Inside AWS Security Agent: A multi-agent architecture for automated penetration testing
item.description: AI agents have traditionally faced three core limitations: they can’t retain learned information or operate autonomously beyond short periods, and they require constant supervision. AWS addresses these limitations wit...

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.

item.title: Aeternum Botnet Shifts Command Control to Polygon Blockchain
item.description: New botnet Aeternum shifted C2 operations to Polygon blockchain, complicating takedown efforts

item.title: Darktrace Flags 32 Million Phishing Emails in 2025 as Identity Attacks Intensify
item.description: 2025 saw 32M phishing emails, with identity threats surpassing vulnerabilities

item.title: Exploitable Vulnerabilities Present in 87% of Organizations
item.description: Datadog report reveals two-fifths of services are affected by exploitable bugs

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Urge Immediate Patching of Cisco SD-WAN Zero Day
item.description: The US and allies are urging Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN customers to hunt for signs of exploitation

item.title: CrowdStrike FalconID Brings Phishing-Resistant MFA to Falcon Next-Gen Identity Security
Flaws in Claude Code Put Developers' Machines at Risk
item.description: The vulnerabilities highlight a big drawback to integrating AI into software development workflows and the potential impact on supply chains.

item.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
item.description: Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.

item.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.

item.title: Malicious Next.js Repos Target Developers Via Fake Job Interviews
item.description: Linked to North Korean fake job-recruitment campaigns, the poisoned repositories are aimed at establishing persistent access to infected machines.

item.title: 44% Surge in App Exploits as AI Speeds Up Cyber-Attacks, IBM Finds
item.description: IBM's 2026 X-Force report reveals 44% rise in cyber-attacks on public apps, driven by AI and flaws

item.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
item.description: ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2022-20775 Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Path Traversal Vulnerability CVE-2026-20127...

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Guidance for Ongoing Global Exploitation of Cisco SD-WAN Systems
item.description: The purpose of this Alert is to provide resources for organizations with Cisco Software-Defined Wide-Area Networking (SD-WAN) systems, including Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies, to address ongoing ex...

item.title: Cost of Insider Incidents Surges 20% to Nearly $20m
item.description: DTEX claims insider incidents cost $19.5m in 2025, with employee negligence most expensive

item.title: Multifaceted Phishing Scheme Deceives Bitpanda Customers
item.description: Phishing attack mimicking Bitpanda targets users, harvesting credentials and personal information

item.title: North Korean Lazarus Group Expands Ransomware Activity With Medusa
item.description: Ransomware Medusa linked to North Korean hackers targets US healthcare amid ongoing attacks

item.title: Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT Security
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals most OT attacks begin in IT. Learn how edge-driven defense stops threats early and turns dwell time into advantage. The post Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT S...

item.title: AI Accelerates Attacker Breakout Time to Just Four Minutes
item.description: ReliaQuest claims AI has reduced breakout and exfiltration time to under 10 minutes
